1.name: Easy
1.description: Factual questions directly from the text

2.name: Medium
2.description: Interpretive questions about meaning and themes

questions.question: Who did the Israelites ask the LORD about attacking first?

options.A: Who shall go up for us against the Canaanites first?
options.B: Who shall be king over us?
options.D: Where should we build an altar?

questions.explanation: Judges 1:1 records Israel asking the LORD who should go up first against the Canaanites.

questions.question: Which tribe did the LORD say should attack first?
questions.explanation: Judges 1:2 says the LORD appointed Judah to go up first.

questions.question: Who went with Judah to fight in their lot?
questions.explanation: Judges 1:3 tells how Judah asked Simeon to go with them.

questions.question: What was done to Adoni-bezek after he was captured?

options.C: His thumbs and great toes were cut off

questions.explanation: His thumbs and toes were cut off in Judges 1:6.

questions.question: Which city did Judah fight against and take, also slaying Sheshai, Ahiman, and Talmai?
questions.explanation: Judges 1:10 names Hebron as the city taken by Judah.

questions.question: Who promised his daughter Achsah to the man who captured Kirjath-sepher?
questions.explanation: Caleb made the promise in Judges 1:12.

questions.question: Who took Kirjath-sepher and received Achsah as his wife?
questions.explanation: Othniel captured the city and married Achsah in Judges 1:13.

questions.question: What did Achsah ask her father Caleb for after marriage?
questions.explanation: She requested springs of water in Judges 1:14.

questions.question: Why could Judah not drive out the inhabitants of the valley?
questions.explanation: Judges 1:19 explains the valley people had iron chariots.

questions.question: Which tribe failed to drive out the Jebusites from Jerusalem?
questions.explanation: Judges 1:21 states Benjamin did not drive out the Jebusites.

questions.question: What did the man show the house of Joseph when they asked him?
questions.explanation: He showed them the entrance into the city in Judges 1:24.

questions.question: What happened to the man and his family after he showed the entrance into the city?
questions.explanation: Judges 1:25 says they let him and his family go.

questions.question: What did some tribes do when they were strong but did not drive out all the Canaanites?
questions.explanation: Judges 1:28 notes they put Canaanites to tribute.

questions.question: Who came up from Gilgal to Bochim speaking to Israel?
questions.explanation: An angel of the LORD appeared in Judges 2:1.

questions.question: What did the LORD say Israel had failed to do?

options.A: Make no league with the inhabitants of the land and throw down their altars

questions.explanation: Judges 2:2 rebukes them for making leagues and leaving altars standing.

questions.question: What would the nations left in the land become to Israel?
questions.explanation: The nations would become thorns and snares according to Judges 2:3.

questions.question: What name did people give the place where they wept after hearing the angel?
questions.explanation: They named it Bochim in Judges 2:4.

questions.question: What did the Israelites do after Joshua died?

options.B: Went every man unto his inheritance

questions.explanation: Judges 2:6 says they went to possess their inheritances.

questions.question: How long did the people serve the LORD after Joshua and the elders died?

options.A: All the days of Joshua and the elders that outlived him

questions.explanation: They served the LORD while Joshua and the elders lived, per Judges 2:7.

questions.question: What was the condition of the new generation after the old one died?

options.B: They knew not the LORD, nor his works

questions.explanation: Judges 2:10 says the new generation did not know the LORD.

questions.question: What did Israel do in the sight of the LORD?
questions.explanation: Judges 2:11 records Israel doing evil in God's sight.

questions.question: Which gods did Israel begin to serve?
questions.explanation: They served Baalim and Ashtaroth in Judges 2:12.

questions.question: What did the LORD do because of Israel's evil?

options.D: Delivered them into the hands of spoilers and enemies

questions.explanation: God delivered them into the hands of their enemies in Judges 2:14.

questions.question: Who did the LORD raise up to deliver Israel?
questions.explanation: The LORD raised up judges to deliver them in Judges 2:16.

questions.question: Who was raised up to deliver Israel from Chushan-rishathaim?
questions.explanation: Othniel delivered Israel from Mesopotamia in Judges 3:9.

questions.question: What came upon Othniel when he judged Israel?
questions.explanation: The Spirit of the LORD was upon him as described in Judges 3:10.

questions.question: How many years of rest did the land have after Othniel judged Israel?
questions.explanation: The land had rest forty years in Judges 3:11.

questions.question: Who became Israel’s deliverer from Eglon king of Moab?
questions.explanation: Ehud was the deliverer raised up in Judges 3:15.

questions.question: Which hand did Ehud use to draw his dagger?
questions.explanation: Ehud was left-handed and used his left hand in Judges 3:17.

questions.question: Where was Eglon sitting when Ehud approached him?
questions.explanation: Eglon was in a summer parlour in Judges 3:20.

questions.question: What did Ehud do after killing Eglon?
questions.explanation: He shut the doors and escaped in Judges 3:23.

questions.question: How many men of valour did Shamgar kill with an ox goad?
questions.explanation: Shamgar struck down six hundred Philistines with an ox goad in Judges 3:31.

questions.question: Who judged Israel at the time of Deborah?
questions.explanation: Deborah served as judge and prophetess in Judges 4:4.

questions.question: Which general did Deborah call to fight Sisera?
questions.explanation: Deborah sent for Barak in Judges 4:6.

questions.question: How many chariots of iron did Sisera have?
questions.explanation: Sisera commanded nine hundred iron chariots in Judges 4:7.

questions.question: Who did Deborah say would get the honor of victory over Sisera?
questions.explanation: She said the honor would go to a woman in Judges 4:9.

questions.question: What did Deborah tell Barak was the day God would deliver Sisera?

options.A: The LORD hath delivered Sisera into thine hand

questions.explanation: She affirmed God would deliver Sisera into his hand in Judges 4:14.

questions.question: What happened to Sisera when the LORD discomfited him?
questions.explanation: Sisera fled on foot after his army was defeated in Judges 4:15.

questions.question: Where did Jael drive a nail through Sisera's temple?
questions.explanation: Jael killed Sisera in her tent in Judges 4:17.

questions.question: What happened to Jabin king of Canaan?
questions.explanation: Judges 4:24 says Jabin was destroyed as Israel had prevailed over him.

questions.question: Who sang a song of victory after Sisera's defeat?
questions.explanation: Deborah and Barak sang the victory song in Judges 5:1.

questions.question: What happened in Israel since the days of Ehud?

options.B: The highways were unoccupied and travelers walked through byways

questions.explanation: Judges 5:7 describes roads being deserted until the time of Deborah.

questions.question: What did the survivors of Sisera do afterwards?
questions.explanation: Judges 5:31 says survivors came no more again into Israel.

questions.question: Who oppressed Israel for seven years because they did evil in the sight of the LORD?
questions.explanation: The Midianites oppressed Israel for seven years in Judges 6:1.

questions.question: Which judge was threshing wheat in a winepress to hide from the Midianites?
questions.explanation: Gideon was threshing wheat in a winepress in Judges 6:11.

questions.question: What did the angel call Gideon in Judges 6:12?
questions.explanation: The angel called him a mighty man of valour.

questions.question: What did the LORD tell Gideon He would be with him to do?
questions.explanation: God promised to save Israel by Gideon's hand in Judges 6:14.

questions.question: How did Gideon respond to God's call?

options.A: If the LORD be with me, why then is all this

questions.explanation: He expressed doubt to God in Judges 6:16.

questions.question: What did Gideon do to his father's altar of Baal?

options.C: Pulled it down and cut down the grove

questions.explanation: Gideon tore down the altar and cut down the grove in Judges 6:25.

questions.question: What did Gideon do with the second bullock?

options.A: Took it, second bullock, and offered it upon the rock

questions.explanation: Gideon offered the second bullock on the rock according to Judges 6:27.

questions.question: What effect did the death of Joshua and the elders have on Israel's faith?

options.B: A new generation arose that did not know the LORD

questions.explanation: The new generation did not know the LORD after the old leaders died.

questions.question: Why did the LORD strengthen Israel's enemies against them?

options.A: Because Israel forsook the LORD and served Baal and Ashtaroth
options.B: Because they were weak in battle

questions.explanation: Their idolatry caused God to strengthen their foes.

questions.question: What pattern is shown by God raising judges?

options.A: He abandoned Israel after their sin
options.C: He delivered them when they cried out, showing mercy despite their disobedience

questions.explanation: God repeatedly delivered Israel in response to repentance.

questions.question: What does the Spirit coming upon a judge indicate?

options.A: He was chosen by the people
options.D: He had God’s empowerment for deliverance

questions.explanation: The Spirit enabled the judge to lead and deliver Israel.

questions.question: How long did the Israelites serve Eglon king of Moab?
questions.explanation: Judges 3:14 indicates Israel served Eglon eighteen years.

questions.question: What characteristic of Eglon made Ehud's assassination possible?
questions.explanation: Eglon's corpulence and seclusion permitted Ehud's plan.

questions.question: Why is Deborah significant among Israel's judges?

options.A: She was a prophetess and judge

questions.explanation: Deborah combined judicial and prophetic leadership.

questions.question: What does the promise about a woman winning the victory teach?

options.C: God often works through unexpected instruments

questions.explanation: The prophecy shows God can honor unexpected agents of victory.

questions.question: What role did the LORD play in the battle against Sisera?

options.A: He discomfited Sisera and his host
options.C: He sent hail only after the battle
options.D: He gave the victory to Jabin

questions.explanation: God intervened directly in the battle against Sisera.

questions.question: How did Jael fulfil Deborah’s prophecy?
questions.explanation: Jael killed Sisera, fulfilling the prophecy that a woman would prevail.

questions.question: What is the significance of the song of Deborah and Barak?

options.A: It celebrates God's deliverance and commemorates victory

questions.explanation: The song commemorates divine rescue and Israel’s deliverance.

questions.question: What does the final verse of Deborah’s song convey?

options.A: No survivors of Sisera’s host came against Israel anymore

questions.explanation: It states the defeat ended Sisera’s threat permanently.

questions.question: Why did the LORD allow the Midianites to oppress Israel?

options.B: Because the Midianites were stronger by birth
options.C: Because Israel did evil and turned from God
options.D: Because they needed training in war

questions.explanation: Their disobedience resulted in the oppression by Midian.

questions.question: What does the angel’s greeting to Gideon suggest about God’s perspective on him?

options.D: God saw him as a future deliverer despite his fear

questions.explanation: The greeting recognizes Gideon’s potential despite his doubt.

questions.question: What does Gideon offering the second bullock reveal about his faith?

options.A: He was afraid of the people
options.B: He obeyed the LORD despite danger

questions.explanation: Gideon obeyed God even when it endangered him publicly.

questions.question: What did the Spirit of the LORD coming upon Gideon enable him to do?

options.A: Lead and deliver Israel against Midian

questions.explanation: The Spirit empowered him for his role as judge and warrior.

questions.question: Why did God reduce Gideon’s army from thirty-two thousand to three hundred?

options.A: So Israel would know the victory came from God and not numbers

questions.explanation: The reduction demonstrated God's power rather than human might.

questions.question: What strategy did Gideon use against the Midianite camp?

options.C: A surprise attack with torches and trumpets

questions.explanation: He used surprise and noise to cause panic in the enemy camp.

questions.question: What happened when the three hundred blew trumpets and broke the pitchers?
questions.explanation: The enemy panicked and fled at the noise and light.

questions.question: How did the Midianite armies react to hearing about the Israelite attack?

options.B: They turned every man and fled

questions.explanation: They fled when they heard the camp was alarmed.

questions.question: Why did the men of Ephraim confront Gideon?

options.C: For not calling them to battle earlier

questions.explanation: They were upset he didn't call them to battle earlier after the victory.

questions.question: What did the people offer Gideon after victory?

options.A: To make him king over them

questions.explanation: They offered him kingship, but he refused.

questions.question: Why did Gideon refuse to rule as king?

options.B: He said the LORD shall rule over Israel
options.C: He wanted to return to farming

questions.explanation: He acknowledged the LORD as Israel’s ruler.

questions.question: What did Gideon make from the spoils, causing Israel to worship it?
questions.explanation: He made an ephod that became a snare to Israel.

questions.question: What was special about Samson's birth according to the angel?

options.A: He would be a Nazarite from the womb
options.B: He would be born in Egypt

questions.explanation: The angel prophesied Samson would be Nazarite from birth.

questions.question: What were Samson’s parents told not to eat?

options.B: Nothing unclean and no wine or strong drink

questions.explanation: The Nazirite requirements were given to them in Judges 13:7.

questions.question: Why did Samson insist on marrying a Philistine woman?

options.D: Because she was from his tribe

questions.explanation: Samson desired her because she pleased his eyes.

questions.question: What enabled Samson to kill a lion with his bare hands?
questions.explanation: The Spirit of the LORD empowered Samson’s strength.

questions.question: What challenge did Samson give at his wedding feast?
questions.explanation: He challenged them to solve his riddle for thirty garments.

questions.question: What was the consequence when Samson’s wife was given to another man?
questions.explanation: Samson burned their standing grain in revenge.

questions.question: How many Philistines did Samson kill with the jawbone of an ass?
questions.explanation: He killed a thousand men with the jawbone in Judges 15:14.

questions.question: Who was the woman that Samson loved in Gaza?
questions.explanation: Samson loved a woman in Gaza according to Judges 16:4.

questions.question: What did Delilah ask Samson to do to prove his weakness?

options.C: Tell her the secret of his strength

questions.explanation: She wanted him to tell her the secret of his strength.

questions.question: What was the secret of Samson’s strength?

options.A: His hair had never been shorn

questions.explanation: His unshorn hair was the source of his Nazarite strength.

questions.question: What did Delilah do after cutting Samson's hair?

options.B: Called the Philistines to bind him

questions.explanation: She called the Philistines to capture him after his strength left.

questions.question: What happened to Samson after the Philistines captured him?

options.C: They put out his eyes and made him grind in prison

questions.explanation: He was blinded and made to grind in prison.

questions.question: Why did the Philistines gather to offer a great sacrifice to Dagon?

options.A: Because they thought their god had delivered Samson into their hand

questions.explanation: They celebrated Dagon after capturing Samson.

questions.question: What did Samson pray for as he stood between the pillars?

options.C: For strength to avenge himself on the Philistines

questions.explanation: He asked God for strength to defeat the Philistines one more time.

questions.question: What was the outcome of Samson pushing the pillars?

options.B: The house fell and many Philistines died

questions.explanation: The temple collapsed, killing many Philistines and Samson.

questions.question: How did Samson die?

options.A: He died when he brought down the house upon himself

questions.explanation: His final act killed him along with his enemies.

questions.question: What phrase describes Israel during the time of Micah?

options.A: Every man did that which was right in his own eyes

questions.explanation: This phrase summarizes Israel’s moral chaos in Judges 17:6.

questions.question: What happened to the house of God that the Danites took?

options.B: They set it up and made it their idol
options.D: They used it as a hospital

questions.explanation: They established it as an idolatrous sanctuary.

questions.question: What city did a Levite and his concubine travel to?
questions.explanation: They went to Gibeah in Judges 19:1.

questions.question: What crime was committed against the Levite's concubine in Gibeah?

options.A: She was abused by men of the city

questions.explanation: The men of Gibeah abused her and she died.

questions.question: What did Israel do after hearing the crime against the concubine?

options.A: Gathered together to ask what should be done

questions.explanation: They convened to decide on punishment.

questions.question: What did Israel demand from Benjamin after Gibeah’s crime?

options.B: The guilty men to be handed over

questions.explanation: They demanded justice for the atrocity.

questions.question: What was the outcome of the second battle with Benjamin?

options.C: Benjamin still prevailed in the second fight

questions.explanation: Benjamin won again in the second battle.

questions.question: How did Israel eventually defeat Benjamin?

options.B: By hiding ambushers in the vineyards

questions.explanation: They used an ambush hidden in vineyards.

questions.question: Why did Israel grieve after the war with Benjamin?

options.D: Because one tribe had almost been destroyed

questions.explanation: They mourned the near destruction of Benjamin.

questions.question: What does Judges 21:25 say about Israel in that time?

options.A: There was no king in Israel; every man did that which was right in his own eyes

questions.explanation: This verse summarizes the spiritual state of Israel in Judges.

questions.question: What can Judah's failure to drive out valley inhabitants tell us about the conquest?

options.B: Conquest was incomplete and depended on obedience

questions.explanation: Their inability shows that remaining enemies was often due to disobedience, not lack of promise.

questions.question: What is the recurring cycle presented in Judges 2?
questions.explanation: Judges depicts a repeated cycle of Israel's faithfulness and failure.

questions.question: Why is Othniel’s deliverance significant in the pattern of judges?

options.A: He was the only judge not chosen by God
options.B: He illustrates God’s mercy in raising deliverers after repentance

questions.explanation: His role fits the pattern of divine rescue when Israel cried out.

questions.question: What does the brief note about Shamgar suggest about leadership in Israel?

options.D: God could use unlikely individuals in crises

questions.explanation: Shamgar’s brief mention highlights God's use of unexpected deliverers.

questions.question: How does Deborah’s role challenge common ancient assumptions about women?

options.A: She demonstrates that God can appoint women as leaders and prophets
options.C: She was a queen in name only

questions.explanation: Deborah’s leadership defies patriarchal expectations of her time.

questions.question: Why was Barak told that a woman would receive the honor of victory?

options.B: Because women fought in the battle
options.C: Because Sisera was afraid of women
options.D: To show God’s control over outcome and honor

questions.explanation: It underscored God’s sovereignty and fulfilled prophecy by Jael’s action.

questions.question: What is the theological importance of the song of Deborah and Barak?

options.A: It introduces a new law code
options.B: It replaces the earlier Exodus song
options.C: It celebrates divine deliverance and warns against forgetting God
options.D: It is a song for harvest

questions.explanation: The song preserves theology and historical memory of God’s act.

questions.question: What does Gideon’s initial reluctance teach about faith and calling?

options.A: God often calls imperfect people and gives grace for their assignment

questions.explanation: Gideon’s doubt shows God uses weak vessels for His purposes.

questions.question: Why did God diminish Gideon’s army before battle?

options.A: To prove victory is from God and not from Israel’s strength
options.D: Because Gideon asked for fewer men

questions.explanation: The reduction prevented Israel from taking glory for the win.

questions.question: What theological lesson is found in Gideon’s refusal to be king?

options.B: True leadership in Israel belonged to the LORD, not a human monarch
options.C: He was too humble to accept reward

questions.explanation: Gideon affirmed God as Israel’s sovereign ruler.

questions.question: How did Gideon’s ephod become a problem?

options.C: It was worshipped as a god by Gideon
options.D: Israel made it an object of idolatry

questions.explanation: The ephod led Israel astray into idolatry despite Gideon’s earlier faithfulness.

questions.question: What does Samson’s Nazirite vow from birth signify?

options.A: His life was set apart for a special divine purpose
options.C: He was to live only in the temple

questions.explanation: The vow marked him as dedicated for deliverance from the womb.

questions.question: What spiritual error led to Samson’s downfall?

options.B: Revealing his consecration secret to Delilah and trusting in his own strength

questions.explanation: Samson’s weakness for Delilah caused him to betray his Nazirite vows.

questions.question: What does Samson’s final act teach about redemption?

options.A: God can use a flawed servant to accomplish His purposes even at the end of life
options.B: Destruction is always the final outcome
options.C: Strength comes from physical power alone

questions.explanation: Even in failure, God used Samson once more for judgment against Israel’s enemies.

questions.question: What is the larger message of the phrase 'every man did that which was right in his own eyes'?

options.C: Moral relativism and chaos reigned due to the lack of righteous leadership

questions.explanation: The phrase captures the disorder and spiritual decline of the era.

questions.question: How does the story of the Danites stealing Micah's idols reflect Israel’s condition?

options.A: They were faithful to the LORD
options.C: They were unified under the judges
options.D: They showed religious confusion and self-serving faith

questions.explanation: The theft of idols demonstrates how misguided Israel’s worship had become.

questions.question: What was the purpose of the Levite cutting his concubine's body into twelve pieces?

options.B: To call all Israel to witness the outrage and seek justice

questions.explanation: It was a shocking act intended to mobilize Israel against sin and injustice.

questions.question: Why did Israel consult the LORD before battling Benjamin?

options.A: To seek God's favor and plan

questions.explanation: They sought divine guidance before war in Judges 20:18.

questions.question: What does Israel’s strategy against Benjamin reveal about warfare in Judges?

options.C: They combined divine guidance with tactical ambush and surprise
options.D: They fought openly in pitched battle only

questions.explanation: They used a tactical ambush along with seeking God’s direction.

questions.question: How did Israel attempt to preserve the tribe of Benjamin after near annihilation?

options.A: They arranged marriages and allowed the Benjamites to take wives from Shiloh
options.D: They forced Benjamin to leave the land

questions.explanation: They found wives for Benjamin to keep the tribe alive.

questions.question: What warning does the final verse of Judges offer believers today?
questions.explanation: The final verse warns against moral relativism when there is no righteous leadership.

questions.question: What role did foreign gods play in Israel's cycles of judgment?

options.A: They were the cause of Israel's oppression because Israel abandoned the LORD
options.B: They were alliances to protect Israel
options.D: They were worshipped alongside the LORD without consequence

questions.explanation: Idolatry led directly to Israel's suffering and judgment.

questions.question: How does the oppression by Eglon compare with the deliverance by Ehud?

options.A: It shows the power of lip service
options.B: It proves Israel always won easily
options.C: It reveals God punished Israel by defeat
options.D: It demonstrates that deliverance comes after repentance and God raises unlikely saviors

questions.explanation: The pattern is that God mercifully raises deliverers after Israel repents.

questions.question: What does Barak’s hesitation to go without Deborah suggest about human dependence on divine leadership?

options.B: People often need visible divine confirmation before acting
options.D: God does not want anyone to act

questions.explanation: Barak sought Deborah’s presence as assurance of God's provision.

questions.question: Why is Gideon’s reduced army still successful?

options.A: Because God caused confusion among the enemy, proving the victory was divine
options.B: Because the enemy had no weapons

questions.explanation: The victory highlights God's power over military advantage.

questions.question: What tension is present in Gideon’s refusal of kingship and the later idolatry he introduced?

options.A: He was both humble and faithful throughout
options.B: He recognized God’s rule but still struggled with personal compromise

questions.explanation: Gideon’s story shows that even godly leaders can make mistakes.

questions.question: What does the promise of Samson’s birth teach about God’s use of miraculous beginnings?

options.C: God often prepares deliverers through miraculous births

questions.explanation: Samson’s birth points to God’s sovereign preparation of leaders.

questions.question: How does Samson’s secret being tied to his hair symbolize his spiritual condition?

options.B: His devotion and commitment were external, not internalized
options.D: He trusted himself more than God

questions.explanation: His secret shows how outward vows can mask inner failure.

questions.question: Why is the repeated refrain in Judges significant for the book’s theme?

options.A: It emphasizes the moral decline and need for faithful leadership

questions.explanation: The refrain highlights the spiritual chaos of the period.

questions.question: How might the Danite story serve as a warning about religious innovation?

options.A: It warns that seeking convenient worship leads to idolatry
options.C: It says all innovations are good

questions.explanation: The Danites’ idolatry shows the danger of departing from God’s commands.

questions.question: What broader social problem does the Levite’s concubine story highlight?

options.D: Lawlessness and disregard for human dignity

questions.explanation: The story reveals how moral breakdown leads to atrocity and national crisis.

questions.question: What does consulting the LORD before war demonstrate about Israel’s values?

options.A: They still sought God’s guidance even in national crisis
options.B: They trusted only their own strength

questions.explanation: They recognized the need for divine direction in judgment and war.

questions.question: Why did Israel make vows at Bethel and Mizpah after the war with Benjamin?

options.B: To preserve the remaining tribes and restore the nation

questions.explanation: They vowed to save Israel from extinction and right the wrongs.

questions.question: What is the danger of not having a central godly authority, according to Judges 21:25?

options.A: People decide right and wrong for themselves

questions.explanation: The verse warns that subjective morality leads to chaos.

questions.question: What does the brief account of Shamgar with an ox goad tell us about victory in Judges?

options.D: God can use simple tools through chosen servants

questions.explanation: Unexpected deliverers and methods appear repeatedly in Judges.

questions.question: What does Deborah's statement 'Is not the LORD gone out before thee?' teach about courage?

options.A: Courage is rooted in God’s prior action, not self-confidence
options.B: Courage is a human trait only

questions.explanation: She reminds Barak that God has already gone before them.

questions.question: How does the greeting 'The LORD is with thee, thou mighty man of valour' affect Gideon’s identity?

options.A: It makes him famous among enemies
options.C: It reshapes him from fearful to chosen deliverer

questions.explanation: God’s declaration reframes Gideon’s self-understanding toward purpose.

questions.question: What inconsistency appears in Gideon’s story after his military triumph?

options.A: He refuses kingship but later causes idolatry

questions.explanation: His later actions show lingering spiritual weakness despite divine deliverance.

questions.question: What theological warning does Samson’s exposure of his strength secret convey?

options.A: Secrets are always safe with loved ones
options.B: Trusting in outward vows or personal secrets rather than God can lead to downfall

questions.explanation: The story warns against misplaced trust in personal strength.

questions.question: What is the meaning of Samson's final prayer and action?

options.A: It is an act of repentance and surrender to God's purpose
options.B: It is a request for more wealth
options.C: It is a cry for revenge only
options.D: It is a curse upon Israel

questions.explanation: His last act was both judgement on the enemy and submission to God’s will.

questions.question: How does the house of God becoming an idol in Dan illustrate the danger of religious syncretism?

options.B: It proved God liked new shrines
options.C: It demonstrates that mixing true worship with pagan practices leads to outright idolatry

questions.explanation: It shows how the boundary between devotion and idolatry can be blurred when people justify unbiblical worship.

questions.question: Why was the Levite's action of dismembering his concubine especially shocking?

options.B: It underscored the depth of Israel’s moral collapse and the urgency for justice

questions.explanation: The act was a desperate call to awaken Israel to sin and violence.

questions.question: What does Israel’s question 'Shall we again go to battle?' reveal about their attitude?

options.A: They desired to follow God's direction before action

questions.explanation: They sought confirmation from the LORD before continuing the war.

questions.question: What does Israel's mourning at Mizpah teach about collective responsibility?

options.C: It was a sign of weakness
options.D: It expressed national grief for a brother tribe’s near extinction

questions.explanation: The nation grieved for the loss of Benjamin because of the consequences of sin.

questions.question: Why did Israel spare the remaining Benjamites after making war on Gibeah?

options.B: They did not want to destroy one of Israel’s tribes completely
options.C: They were afraid of foreign nations

questions.explanation: They preserved Benjamin to keep the tribal structure intact.

questions.question: How can Judges 21:25 be read as a critique of Israel’s leadership model?

options.A: It criticizes the lack of a central godly authority and the resulting moral relativism

questions.explanation: The verse highlights the problem of subjective morality without righteous leadership.

questions.question: What does the transfer of Micah’s priest to Dan reveal about religious authority in Judges?

options.A: Priests were always appointed by God
options.B: Religious authority was often illegitimate and based on convenience

questions.explanation: The Danites used whatever priest they could obtain, showing disorder in worship.

questions.question: Why is the role of judges important in the book of Judges?

options.A: They were kings ruling Israel permanently
options.C: They were temporary deliverers raised by God during cycles of apostasy and repentance
options.D: They were prophets who wrote scripture

questions.explanation: Judges delivered Israel from specific oppressors at critical moments.

questions.question: How does God calling Gideon a 'mighty man of valour' despite his fear relate to Christian discipleship?

options.A: It shows God calls and strengthens those He chooses, not based on their present confidence
options.B: It means only strong people can serve God
options.C: It implies fear disqualifies a leader
options.D: It requires wealth to be used by God

questions.explanation: God often calls people by their future identity rather than present condition.

questions.question: What is the deeper meaning of the LORD being 'gone out before thee' in Deborah's message?

options.C: God wanted them to rely on themselves
options.D: God had already determined to deliver them, so they should trust His initiative

questions.explanation: It reassures that the battle was already won in principle because God was acting first.
